one_line_positioning: AI Product Engineer with proven success building and shipping consumer AI products from zero to engaged users, delivering cost and performance optimizations at scale
narrative_angle: Built and shipped a consumer AI product (Emote) from zero to 450+ users with strong retention, reducing LLM costs by 98% — a rare blend of technical depth, product ownership, and business focus that a CEO’s office role demands. While lacking direct front-end and retail experience, demonstrated ability to master any tech stack and domain quickly in high-ambiguity environments.
lead_strengths: Built Emote from zero to 450+ users with 40+ power users and 13-15% weekly retention without paid acquisition.
Reduced LLM inference cost per conversation by ~98% (from $1.20 to $0.023) through model selection, caching, and prompt optimization.
Designed multi-agent orchestration layer enabling AI-assisted quality and control workflows for engineering teams.
proof_points_to_emphasize: Reduced LLM inference cost per conversation by ~98% (from $1.20 to $0.023) through model selection, caching, and prompt optimization.
Reduced AI response latency from ~15s to ~3s through pipeline optimization and streaming.
Built Emote from zero to 450+ users with 40+ power users and 13-15% weekly retention without paid acquisition.
things_to_downplay: No front-end development experience (disqualifier per JD)
No e-commerce or retail domain experience
tone_guidance: Outcome-first, emphasize metrics and ownership. Use 'built', 'improved', 'delivered'. Avoid AI jargon like 'agentic' or 'multi-agent'; reframe as orchestration or collaborative systems. Highlight user-centric metrics and cost savings to resonate with e-commerce personalization goals.
recruiter_first_impression_target: This candidate has the rare ability to not only build AI systems but also drive product outcomes and cost efficiency — exactly what our CEO's office needs to push personalization initiatives, even if they lack retail background.
hiring_manager_objection: No front-end development experience, which is a disqualifier per the JD, and no e-commerce or retail background to understand customer personalization.
objection_preempt: While I haven't worked in retail or front-end, my track record of building a consumer AI product from scratch and delivering 98% cost reduction shows I can quickly master any tech stack and domain to drive business impact in a CEO's office context.
reasoning: The candidate has strong AI engineering and product ownership experience aligning with core AI and business impact needs, but lacks front-end development (a disqualifier) and e-commerce domain familiarity. The stance is STRETCH because the strengths are compelling but the front-end gap may screen them out automatically. The narrative pivots to transferable skills, fast learning, and proven ability to deliver in ambiguous, ownership-heavy roles.
